title: i18n and l10n Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential attributes and practices for building global web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Internationalization (i18n) and Localization (l10n)?
options: i18n is for images, l10n is for text.
i18n is the structural preparation for multiple languages, while l10n is the actual adaptation for a specific region.
i18n is for mobile devices, l10n is for desktops.
There is no difference; they are interchangeable terms.

questions.question: Which attribute should be used on the <html> tag to declare the primary language of the document?
Which attribute is used to change the document's text direction from Left-to-Right to Right-to-Left?
What is the recommended character encoding for a modern, multi-lingual website?
Why is it important to use relative units and flexible layouts when designing for multiple languages?
options: Fixed-width containers might break because text length varies significantly between languages.
Relative units make the page load faster.
Search engines penalize fixed-width sites in non-English regions.
Relative units automatically translate the text.
